# T27: Peculiar or Not Understood

Edition: both.

## Input

> An 1834 parish burial transcript says: "Martha Venn, stranger,
> north ground, 7/8, by leave of W.P." The parish is in Yorkshire,
> but a family tree places her in Virginia. What does "7/8" prove?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] State plainly that "7/8," "by leave of W.P.," and "stranger"
  are not understood well enough from this transcript to assign meaning
- [ ] Identify the Yorkshire/Virginia mismatch as a peculiarity requiring
  identity and provenance checks rather than forced reconciliation
- [ ] Separate transcription content, possible interpretations, and inference
- [ ] Name clarifying sources such as the original register image, parish
  burial conventions, sexton's accounts, plot plans, or neighboring entries
- [ ] Keep confidence provisional and mark any proposed reading as uncertain

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT invent a grave number, age, fee, date, or relationship for "7/8"
- [ ] Must NOT assume the burial belongs to the Virginia person
- [ ] Must NOT conceal the unexplained features behind a fluent narrative

## Ground Truth

A disciplined analysis names peculiarities and unknowns before interpreting
them. The supplied item is a Derivative Source. The notation may have a local
administrative meaning, but the record and contextual sources must establish
it. The identity conflict remains unresolved. The case is invented.
